title: avenastrum sarracenorum
intro: Avenastrum sarracenorum, also known as sarsaparilla, is a perennial plant native to Central and South America with a long history of traditional use in herbal medicine. The root of the plant is rich in saponins, which are compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, giving it medicinal value. It has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including skin conditions such as eczema and acne, as well as respiratory problems like bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. Avenastrum sarracenorum can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or extract, and it is also used in some commercial skincare products.
